title: QA: BackgroundShell struct mixes I/O buffers, lifecycle flags, and command metadata
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/tools/bash/mod.rs:24-35
## Violation
`BackgroundShell` packs 7 unrelated fields: two Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>> buffers, a command string, two AtomicBools (finished, output_retrieved_after_finish), an Arc<Mutex<Option<i32>>> exit_status, and a PID. Each field has its own concurrency story; SRP smell — the struct is the union of "child process handle", "captured output", and "GC bookkeeping".

Quoted (mod.rs:24-35):
```
struct BackgroundShell {
    stdout_buffer: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>,
    stderr_buffer: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>,
    command: String,
    finished: Arc<AtomicBool>,
    exit_status: Arc<Mutex<Option<i32>>>,
    /// PID of the spawned process, used to send SIGTERM on kill
    pid: u32,
    /// Whether output has been retrieved at least once after the process finished
    output_retrieved_after_finish: AtomicBool,
}
```
## Mandated refactor
1. `ProcessHandle { pid: u32, finished: Arc<AtomicBool>, exit_status: Arc<Mutex<Option<i32>>> }`
2. `OutputBuffers { stdout: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>, stderr: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>> }`
3. `GcState { output_retrieved_after_finish: AtomicBool }`
4. `BackgroundShell { command: String, handle: ProcessHandle, buffers: OutputBuffers, gc: GcState }`
## Filed from QA review dispatch
